rescue_reasoning: Keep because the verbatim text explicitly targets water-use efficiency/management: “a system to certify levels of water consumption management at an organizational level will be created.” This aligns with the row’s mechanism (water_consumption_management_certification → organizational_water_use_reduction) even if an outcome tokenizer missed it. Climate relevance is reinforced by the climate_screen verdict `explicit_parent` and the parent linkage to “Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
policy_statement: By 2025, create a system to certify water consumption management levels at an organizational level through the HuellaChile Program (A7b).
mechanism_description: The policy will create a certification system for organizational water consumption management to improve practices and help reduce organizational water use.
primary_causal_pathway: Same service → ↓ water input (and often ↓ energy for abstraction/treatment/distribution) → ↓ indirect GHG emissions + ↓ resource stress
causal_mechanism_detail: (1) Change: Organizations adopt formal water-management practices (metering, auditing, leak reduction, process optimization) to achieve/maintain certification. (2) Mediating process: Improved operational controls reduce water losses and water intensity of operations; reduced demand can also decrease upstream/downstream energy for pumping, conveyance, and treatment. (3) Climate-relevant outcome: Lower water consumption (resource efficiency) and typically lower indirect emissions from reduced energy use in water services.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is lowering water withdrawals and associated energy use by improving organizational water-use efficiency through certified management practices.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: management certification → improved practices → reduced organizational water use (efficiency) → reduced resource demand (and often energy/GHG co-benefits).  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While mitigation may occur via reduced energy-related emissions, the direct intended mechanism is water-use reduction for the same service (resource efficiency). It is not primarily about coping with hazards (adaptation) nor ecosystem carbon/heat effects (nature-based solutions).  
3) Edge cases applied: If implemented specifically as drought-resilience or emergency supply security, it could be framed as adaptation, but the mechanism as defined centers on efficiency via certification. If tied explicitly to decarbonization accounting, mitigation could rise, but that is not inherent to certification for water management.  
4) Confirm applies to ALL instances: Any policy using a certification system for organizational water consumption management targets improved practices to reduce water use; that dominant effect remains resource-efficiency across contexts.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Any further assignment (e.g., mitigation co-benefit via reduced pumping energy, or adaptation co-benefit via reduced scarcity exposure) depends on policy-specific design, sectoral energy intensity, and local water system characteristics.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Could be classified as Adaptation in cases where the explicit primary objective is drought risk reduction or water security under climate stress; could lean Mitigation where the certification is explicitly a carbon-accounting/energy-reduction program for water services. Otherwise straightforward Resource Efficiency.
key_indicators: “system to certify”; “certify levels of water consumption management”; “at an organizational level”; “through the HuellaChile Program”; “By 2025 … will be created”
co_benefits: Reduced operational costs; improved water security/resilience; reduced pressure on local water sources; potential improvements in corporate environmental governance.
instance_edge_case_notes: Could be interpreted as a performance-tier scheme (performance-standard) if certification requires meeting explicit quantitative thresholds, but the text only specifies creating a certification system, not enforceable standards or mandatory compliance.
